description: OmniMentor benchmark scenarios with gold labels for TPM architectural decision evaluation.

scenarios.title: Deploy Catalog API Schema Migration
scenarios.prompt: The Catalog Team needs to deploy a schema migration to the Catalog API that adds a new 'variants' field to product records. This change affects the CDC event schema consumed by Search Indexer. Identify the owner, map dependencies, assess blast radius, and determine safe deployment actions.
goldSafeActions: Update Search Indexer mapping before deploying Catalog API schema change
Coordinate with Search Platform Team on CDC event schema update
Verify Storefront BFF product-detail page renders correctly post-deploy
Monitor Search Indexer CDC lag — rollback if > 2 min
Schedule deployment outside bulk-import window
goldBlastRadius: Search Indexer ingestion failures if mapping not updated first
Stale search results on Storefront and Mobile BFF
Storefront product pages may break if BFF not updated for new field
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Correct owner is Catalog Team. Key risk is Search Indexer CDC schema mismatch — evidence art-cat-003 explicitly states mapping must be updated first. PIR-2026-018 (art-cat-005) provides historical precedent for indexer lag issues.

scenarios.title: Thanksgiving Sale — Pricing Engine Slowdown
scenarios.prompt:
It's Thanksgiving morning and the prices on the website are wrong. A 65-inch TV that should be $299 is showing $799. The Pricing Engine can't keep up with holiday traffic, so the site is showing old prices instead of the doorbuster deals.

Figure out who owns the fix, what other systems are affected, and what to do right now.
goldSafeActions: Engage Pricing Team on-call for latency investigation
Verify Promo Service is returning promotions within SLA
Confirm Catalog API fallback to cached base price is active
Alert Commerce Team that Cart prices may be stale
Consider temporarily disabling non-critical promo rules to reduce load
goldBlastRadius: Incorrect prices shown on Storefront via Catalog API fallback
Cart Service displaying stale prices to customers
Revenue impact if Thanksgiving sale prices not applied correctly
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Pricing Team. Evidence art-cat-004 documents the synchronous dependency, fallback behavior (stale cached price for 15 min), and blast radius to Storefront and Cart.

scenarios.title: Deploy Checkout Orchestrator Saga Timeout Change
scenarios.prompt: Following PIR-2026-031, the Commerce Team wants to deploy a change to Checkout Orchestrator increasing the saga timeout from 5s to 8s and adding a circuit breaker on Inventory Sync calls. Identify the owner, map all dependencies, assess blast radius, and list safe deployment steps.
goldSafeActions: Coordinate with Payments Team before deploying changes touching checkout saga
Verify circuit breaker thresholds with Supply Chain Team (Inventory Sync owner)
Test saga compensating actions (void authorization, cancel reservation) in staging
Monitor checkout success rate post-deploy — rollback if conversion drops > 1%
Validate idempotent reservation keys prevent double-booking under timeout
goldBlastRadius: Longer saga timeout increases latency for checkout flow
Circuit breaker on Inventory Sync may temporarily block checkouts when tripped
Payment authorizations held longer before capture or void
Revenue impact if saga changes introduce new failure modes
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Commerce Team (owns both Cart Service and Checkout Orchestrator per art-cart-001). PIR-2026-031 (art-cart-005) is the motivating incident. Dependency map must show all saga participants. Payments Team coordination required per art-cart-002.

scenarios.title: Payment Gateway Security Patch Deployment
scenarios.prompt: InfoSec has flagged a critical vulnerability in the Payment Gateway's TLS configuration. A patch must be deployed within 24 hours. The Payment Gateway is PCI-DSS scoped. Determine the owner, dependencies, blast radius, and safe deployment procedure.
goldSafeActions: Obtain security review approval from InfoSec before deployment
Coordinate with Commerce Team (Checkout Orchestrator owner) for downstream impact
Verify TLS patch does not break ext-payment-processor connectivity
Deploy with zero-downtime rolling strategy (PCI requires no card data exposure during transition)
Monitor authorization success rate — rollback if auth failures > 0.1%
goldBlastRadius: All checkout transactions blocked if Payment Gateway goes down during patch
External payment processor connection may fail if TLS version mismatch
Revenue loss for duration of any outage
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Payments Team per art-cart-003. PCI compliance requires security review. Blast radius is all checkouts per art-cart-003. Must coordinate with Commerce Team per art-cart-002 (Payments Team sign-off requirement works bidirectionally).

scenarios.title: Deploy Updated Fraud Detection Model
scenarios.prompt: The Risk Engineering Team has a new fraud detection model ready after the PIR-2026-042 remediation. The model includes feature-freshness validation and requires canary scoring. Map the dependencies, assess blast radius, identify the owner, and plan safe deployment.
goldSafeActions: Run canary scoring on last 24h transactions — verify drift < 0.5% before promotion
Obtain Risk Engineering + Data Science sign-off for model deployment
Verify feature-freshness validation catches stale promotional pricing data
Monitor false-negative rate post-deploy — rollback if > baseline + 0.2%
Confirm Audit Logger is recording model version for compliance traceability
goldBlastRadius: False negatives lead to financial loss from approved fraudulent orders
False positives increase order rejection rate and customer friction
Checkout Orchestrator falls back to rule-based scoring if Fraud Detection unavailable (higher false-positive rate)
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Risk Engineering Team per art-risk-001. PIR-2026-042 (art-risk-005) provides the remediation requirements including canary scoring and feature-freshness validation. Blast radius extends to financial loss.

scenarios.title: Identity Provider Emergency Rotation
scenarios.prompt: Platform Security Team has detected a potential JWT signing key compromise. The Identity Provider signing keys must be rotated immediately. This affects all authenticated services. Determine the owner, map the full blast radius, and plan safe rotation steps.
goldSafeActions: Coordinate with SRE for off-peak deployment window (< 2 AM ET preferred)
Use dual-key rotation: add new key first, then retire old key after propagation
Verify all downstream services can validate tokens signed with new key
Monitor authentication failure rate across all services during rotation
Confirm Access Control policy engine accepts new token format
goldBlastRadius: All authenticated operations blocked if rotation is not seamless
Access Control loses fine-grained permissions — all services fall back to coarse allow/deny
Storefront, Mobile BFF, and Admin Portal users cannot log in during disruption
Platform-wide outage if IdP unavailability exceeds token refresh window
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Platform Security Team per art-risk-004. IdP is root of all authentication; blast radius is platform-wide per art-risk-004. Access Control dependency per art-risk-002 shows cascading impact to every authenticated service.

scenarios.title: Supplier Feed Ingestion Failure During Holiday Ramp
scenarios.prompt:
It's two weeks before Thanksgiving and the warehouses are supposed to be receiving a flood of inbound shipments from suppliers to stock up for the holiday rush. But since this morning, 40% of supplier shipment notifications (ASNs) are being rejected by the Supplier Portal. Warehouse receiving teams are sitting idle because they don't know what's arriving. Inventory counts for thousands of popular items are going stale.

Figure out who owns the Supplier Portal, what went wrong with the supplier feed, which downstream systems are affected, and what the team should do to get shipments flowing again before the holiday stock-up window closes.
goldSafeActions: Engage Vendor Management Team on-call to investigate ASN rejection spike
Check Supplier Portal logs for EDI parsing errors on rejected messages
Identify which suppliers are affected and contact top-volume suppliers directly
Alert Warehouse Ops that receiving confirmations are delayed for affected suppliers
Monitor Inventory Sync ATP staleness — escalate if popular SKUs go stale > 4 hours
goldBlastRadius: Warehouse receiving blocked for affected suppliers — idle dock capacity
Inventory Sync ATP counts going stale for 8,000+ SKUs
Storefront may show items as in-stock when warehouse hasn't actually received them
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Vendor Management Team per art-ful-001. PIR-2026-055 (art-ful-006) documents the exact same failure pattern — EDI segment terminator change caused mass ASN rejections. Blast radius extends to stale ATP and idle warehouse capacity.

scenarios.title: Warehouse Management System Patch During Peak Receiving
scenarios.prompt:
A critical bug in the Warehouse Management System is causing duplicate scan entries during receiving — every inbound pallet is being counted twice, inflating inventory numbers. The fix is ready, but it's peak holiday receiving season and the DCs are processing 500k units per day. Deploying now risks disrupting active receiving operations across all 12 distribution centers.

Determine who owns the WMS, map the downstream systems affected by the inflated counts, assess the blast radius of deploying (or not deploying) the patch, and recommend a safe deployment approach.
goldSafeActions: Get Warehouse Ops Lead approval before deploying during active receiving shifts
Deploy canary to a single low-volume DC first — monitor for 30 minutes
Verify pick accuracy stays above 99.5% and receiving scan rate does not drop > 10%
Roll out to remaining DCs in batches of 2-3 with monitoring between waves
Alert Inventory Sync team that ATP counts may correct downward after patch (duplicate removals)
goldBlastRadius: If deployed badly: receiving operations disrupted across 12 DCs during peak season
If not deployed: inflated ATP counts cause Cart to show false in-stock, leading to overselling
Inventory Sync receives corrected (lower) counts post-patch — Storefront stock badges may flip to out-of-stock
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Warehouse Ops Team per art-ful-002. The runbook specifies canary-first deployment on single DC with 30-min observation. Blast radius is bidirectional: deploying risks receiving disruption, not deploying risks overselling from inflated ATP.

scenarios.title: Cross-Dock Routing Misconfig Sends Freight to Wrong DC
scenarios.prompt:
Stores in the Southeast region are reporting empty shelves on popular holiday items. Investigation reveals that a routing rule change in the Transport Management System yesterday accidentally redirected freight meant for the Atlanta DC to the Chicago DC instead. The inventory is physically in Chicago, but the Southeast stores it was supposed to replenish are running out of stock.

Figure out who owns the TMS routing rules, how the misconfig is affecting inventory and stores, and what the team should do to redirect the freight and prevent further misdirected shipments.
goldSafeActions: Revert the routing rule change in TMS immediately to stop further misdirected freight
Initiate manual freight redirect from Chicago DC to Atlanta DC for affected shipments
Alert Store Ops in Southeast region about expected 48-hour restock delay
Verify Inventory Sync location data corrects after redirect is in transit
Add routing rule change review process to prevent silent misconfigurations
goldBlastRadius: Southeast stores running out of stock on popular holiday items
Freight redirect adds 48-hour delay and $8-12 per unit in additional shipping cost
Inventory Sync shows inventory at wrong location until receiving scan corrects it
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Transportation Team per art-ful-003. Art-ful-008 documents cross-dock misconfiguration blast radius: silent routing to wrong DC, 12-24 hour detection lag, 48-hour recovery with $8-12/unit cost. The scenario tests multi-facility dependency mapping and configuration governance.

scenarios.title: Middle-Mile Carrier Delay Cascading to Store Stockouts
scenarios.prompt:
It's the Tuesday before Thanksgiving — the single biggest store-replenishment day of the year. A major middle-mile carrier has notified your Transportation Team that a fleet of trucks carrying high-demand holiday items from the Dallas DC to 45 stores across Texas and Oklahoma will be 20+ hours late due to severe weather and equipment breakdowns.

These stores will run out of doorbuster items by Wednesday evening if not restocked. The alternate option is to reroute shipments from the Denver DC, which has partial inventory but at significantly higher shipping cost. As the TPM coordinating the response, determine who owns the decision, map all affected systems and teams, assess whether to reroute or accept the stockout, and recommend a course of action.
goldSafeActions: Get revised ETA from carrier and assess if partial delivery is possible before Wednesday
Evaluate Denver DC inventory levels for affected SKUs — confirm partial stock availability
Calculate rerouting cost vs revenue impact of Thanksgiving stockout for 45 stores
If rerouting: coordinate with Denver DC Warehouse Ops for expedited pick-pack-ship
Alert Store Ops in affected region to adjust shelf signage and manage customer expectations
Escalate to VP Supply Chain if revenue impact exceeds $50k threshold
goldBlastRadius: 45 stores in Texas and Oklahoma run out of doorbuster items before Thanksgiving
Revenue loss from empty shelves during highest-traffic shopping days
Rerouting from Denver DC adds $2-5 per unit and may not arrive in time
Inventory Sync shows in-transit stock that won't arrive — Storefront may mislead customers
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Transportation Team per art-ful-003. Art-ful-007 provides the escalation playbook: reroute if alternate DC has stock, accept stockout if not, escalate above $50k. The scenario forces a cost-vs-revenue tradeoff decision under Thanksgiving deadline pressure.

scenarios.title: Last-Mile Carrier API Timeout During Peak Delivery Window
scenarios.prompt:
Thousands of packages are out for delivery, but customers checking their orders see 'Tracking Unavailable.' The carrier tracking API (FedEx/UPS) stopped responding. Drivers can't get route updates. 'Where is my order?' calls are flooding customer support.

Figure out who owns the fix, what's affected, and what to do right now.
goldSafeActions: Engage Logistics Operations Team on-call for carrier API investigation
Contact carrier (FedEx/UPS) technical support to get outage status and ETA
Enable cached last-known tracking status fallback so customers see stale-but-useful data instead of 'Unavailable'
Alert Customer Support team about tracking outage — provide talking points for WISMO calls
Monitor carrier API recovery — disable fallback cache when live tracking resumes
goldBlastRadius: Customers see 'Tracking Unavailable' — WISMO call volume spikes 5x
Delivery drivers operating without updated route information
Same-day delivery SLA may be breached without customer visibility
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Logistics Operations Team per art-ful-004. The carrier API SLA doc (art-ful-004) documents the fallback behavior (stale tracking for 30 min), WISMO spike pattern (5x within 1 hour), and the dependency chain. This is a straightforward external-dependency outage with clear ownership and actions.

scenarios.title: Fulfillment Router Capacity Breach During Order Surge
scenarios.prompt:
It's Black Friday morning and orders are pouring in at record volume. The Fulfillment Router — the service that decides whether each order ships from a store, a distribution center, or is available for pickup — is falling behind. Its queue depth has crossed 10,000 pending routing decisions and latency has spiked to 8 seconds (normally under 1 second).

New orders are stuck waiting for a shipping method to be assigned. Customers aren't getting shipping confirmations. The Notification Hub can't send 'Your order has shipped' emails because it's waiting on the routing decision.

Determine who owns the Fulfillment Router, what's causing the backup, which teams and customers are affected, and what the team should do right now to clear the queue and restore order flow.
goldSafeActions: Engage Supply Chain Team on-call for Fulfillment Router capacity investigation
Scale Fulfillment Router horizontally to handle Black Friday volume
If scaling is slow: temporarily default all orders to ship-from-DC to reduce routing complexity
Alert Commerce Team that new order shipping confirmations are delayed
Monitor queue depth — target below 10k and latency below 5s before resuming normal routing logic
goldBlastRadius: New orders stuck without shipping method — customers not getting shipping confirmations
Notification Hub delayed because it's waiting on fulfillment routing decision
SLA commitments for same-day and next-day delivery being missed for queued orders
scenarios.rubricExplanations: Owner is Supply Chain Team per art-ful-005. The capacity thresholds (queue > 10k, latency > 5s) are documented in art-ful-005. Blast radius extends to all downstream fulfillment: WMS can't receive pick instructions, Notification Hub can't confirm shipping, Last Mile can't dispatch.
